question.title: PKCS11 error at KeyStore.getEntry(alias, null) at Java 22 statement

answers.body:
This could be "not-related" to your inquiry, but i've ran into problems with new cards which only support EC and not RSA and had to do a proper division and find which card i'm working with before doing anything with them, here's the code excerpt that i hope can be useful for you:

XMLSignatureFactory fac;
fac = XMLSignatureFactory.getInstance("DOM");

KeyStore.Builder builder = KeyStore.Builder.newInstance("PKCS11", providerPKCS11, new KeyStore.CallbackHandlerProtection(cmdLineHdlr));

KeyStore keystore = builder.getKeyStore();
Key key1 = keystore.getKey(assinaturaCertifLabel1, null);

if ("RSA".equalsIgnoreCase(key1.getAlgorithm())) {
    System.out.println("I AM RSA AND GOING");

    signedInfo = fac.newSignedInfo(
        fac.newCanonicalizationMethod(
            CanonicalizationMethod.EXCLUSIVE, // CanonicalizationMethod.EXCLUSIVE_WITH_COMMENTS,
            (C14NMethodParameterSpec) null
        ),
        fac.newSignatureMethod(
            "http://www.w3.org/2001/04/xmldsig-more#rsa-sha256",
            null
        ),
        Collections.singletonList(ref)
    );

} else if ("EC".equals(key1.getAlgorithm())) {
    System.out.println("I AM EC AND GOING");

    signedInfo = fac.newSignedInfo(
        fac.newCanonicalizationMethod(
            CanonicalizationMethod.EXCLUSIVE, // CanonicalizationMethod.EXCLUSIVE_WITH_COMMENTS,
            (C14NMethodParameterSpec) null
        ),
        fac.newSignatureMethod(
            "http://www.w3.org/2001/04/xmldsig-more#ecdsa-sha256",
            null
        ),
        Collections.singletonList(ref)
    );

} else {
    throw new InvalidAlgorithmParameterException("ALGORITHM IS NOT RSA OR EC");
}

comments.body: Thanks for your sharing Fabio, but my code stucks at statement "Key key1 = keystore.getKey(certAlias, null);". Gets "unsupported parameters" error. I think it can be related with config of pkcs11 provider definition.
